name: aar-engineering
description: The SWE pipeline for building the AAR product (the engineering layer, not a shipped research plugin). ship-change drives a scaffold change through a GitHub-backed lifecycle: Issue → worktree branch → namespaced design doc → draft PR → --scaffold design review (posted) → implement → --code review (posted) → classifier (records mechanical|architectural with evidence) → tracked .aar-ci checks + fake-HOME behavior smoke → fail-closed merge-when-clean. Worktree-from-the-start (no shared-checkout races). The cross-family --code review posts a NATIVE GitHub review through a separate reviewer identity (WF_REVIEWER_TOKEN_CMD) — Approve only at finish after the full gate, request-changes/comment during iteration — so branch protection can require a real opposite-family approval; falls back to comments when no reviewer identity is set. Pairs with verify-claims (the review primitives) and a per-repo .aar-ci/ profile.
